test_summary.verify: SUCCESS — UG470 frame-level compare with ECC/BRAM masking
test_summary.fix_applied: bitrev() in CFG_OUT read functions — all config reads now working

cable.type: Xilinx DLC10 Platform Cable USB II (new)
cable.cpld_note: 0xFFFE is normal for DLC10 clones — all config reads work despite this value

readback_issue.suspected_cause: RESOLVED: .bit header alignment + ECC/BRAM volatile bits (UG470)
readback_issue.fix: cmd_verify now finds FDRI write, skips pad frame, masks ECC word #50, tracks BRAM frames separately

resolved_blockers.issue: CPLD version 0xFFFE — was thought to mean dead TDO
resolved_blockers.resolution: New DLC10 cable also reads 0xFFFE but ALL config register reads work perfectly. CPLD 0xFFFE is normal for clones.

resolved_blockers.issue: bitrev() missing in CFG_OUT read functions
resolved_blockers.symptom: Config register reads returned wrong values (STAT=0x02089E3F, IDCODE=0xC8C608C9)
resolved_blockers.resolution: Added bitrev() to read_cfg_out_32() and read_cfg_out_64() functions. TDO comes bit-reversed like TDI.

resolved_issues.title: Verify alignment mismatch — FIXED
resolved_issues.resolution: cmd_verify rewritten: FDRI offset detection, pad frame skip, ECC word #50 masking, BRAM frame tracking per UG470
